subject: [Pre-read] Reg CB LFF/ CNLS/ Projects Monthly Update - Jan 2026
deck_filename: Reg CB LFF_CNLS_Projects Monthly Catchup - Jan_vf2.pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please find attached slides for our discussion tomorrow, let me know for
any questions.

Thank you.

-- 
*Jason YU*
*Regional CB | MY & SG CB*
*Mobile*: +65 90284825
*Email*: jason.yu@shopee.com

Hi Ian,

Please use V2 of the deck attached.

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 5

   - For CN, we still expect 3PF to be so much bigger than FBS?

Is there a
   reason why?
- For markets like TH and PH, the gap is especially wide?

What is the
   main criteria that we are missing here that we cannot address?
Is it size?
Is it ADIS cutoff?

I like to understand the difference in why
   some markets can do, some cannot
   - For KR LFF %, who did we verify this with in KR?

Slide 7

   - For SG and MY, it is still very heavily on seller side?

What is the
   reason this is so?

Slide 8

   - Can relook the numbers for SG and MY?

Feels like quite a bit of
   disconnect compared with what we give to sellers for TH and PH. If we need
   to give more seller discount, it would be more for new FBS sellers and for
   a limited time. It is probably time to wean off for old FBS sellers



Slide 9

   - All these are still seller facing discounts. Should we not position
   this as order driven, so we should be giving them FBS vouchers instead to
   help them grow their sales. I rather give them PRM as compared to having
   revenue foregone. This will drive sales





Slide 15

   - If sellers use SLS, they will have their own cost too?
- Why is there BS stock for staging in Anbo?

Who will make the call if
   the stock in Anbo goes to local?

Slide 16

   - I do not quite understand how to read this table



Slide 17 – 21

   - Probably best to go through this, not quite sure



Slide 27

   - How about CNCB?
How about Taobao push?

What will the key objective be
   for 2026?

Slide 36

   - For the 264 sellers in 2025, which sites did they go to?

Can also
   split the organic and inorganic?

- I remember we wanted to push harder for MY before, where did we land
   there?

Slide 42

   - For those who expanded into MY, how are we getting them to sell into
   SG as well?
The overlap could potentially be high?

Slide 46

   - ERP SKU sourcing has not been good in general?

Slide 47

   - Yet to pitch and uncontactable is probably way too high?

Hi all,

Please see follow up items from our catchup yesterday, let me know for any
questions.

No. Topic Action Items PIC DDL
1 FBS buyer/seller investment CPO Review FBS investment to buyer facing
especially in SG and MY such as changing penetration program rewards from
handling fee discount to shop vouchers

Follow up as of 15th Jan: Sync-ed with SG/MYBI and FBS teams and updated
buyer/seller CPO ratio based on latest platform PRM plan and changing CB
penetration program to order driving vs foregone revenue driven
SG buyer to seller CPO% is 80% to 20%
MY buyer to seller CPO% is 65% to 35% Jason Done
2 CB FBS 2026 Growth plan Re-assess TH / PH LFF targets to factor in
addressable pool assessment and FBS target to further outgrow 3PF Jason 23
Jan
3 Anbo WH Model 1. Aligned to pitch to short-listed sellers and conduct
small scale pilot to validate cost assumption and test end to end operation
2. Review true cost assumptions, and embed Anbo SLS fulfilment into Anbo
staging model (fulfilment to prioritize local CBWH, while switching to Anbo
SLS when OOS in local CBWH)
3. Use 1 rate card for all markets to simplify pitching
4. Clarify findings based on a) True Cost to Serve, b) Profitability to
Shopee, c) Seller Cost of Doing Business Jason 30 Jan
4 Site Expansion Break down organic vs. inorganic uplift by market Ray 16
Jan
5 MY CNLS - SG Direct Scan MY CNLS sellers adoption for SG direct and
assess the opportunity to grow Ray/Jason 23 Jan
6 ERP Pitching Plan Prepare and align on seller acquisition and ERP plan
for 2026 Shuning 16 Jan

content.question:
thanks

for #3, can we align on the true costs and the value we really bring to
sellers before embarking on the pitching?
